siteDescription: Portfolio of Jordan Latta, a Technical Artist specializing in high-performance rendering, shader engineering, and technical pipelines.

backgroundInfo.name: Jordan Latta
backgroundInfo.aboutBlurb: I am a Technical Artist working at the intersection of complex systems engineering and visual artistry. I specialize in designing toolsets, optimizing runtime performance, and building decoupled execution pipelines. From global network architectures to high-fidelity visual rendering, I bridge design intention with production-grade engineering reliability.

experience.title: Lead Technical Artist
details: Engineered modular procedural toolsets reducing landscape creation timelines by 35%.
Profiled and optimized memory usage, clearing runtime bottlenecks for target hardware platforms.
Designed automated asset validation scripts running via CI/CD pipelines.

experience.title: Technical Artist
details: Authored custom post-processing graphics shaders and screen-space asset behaviors.
Maintained core DCC-to-engine asset ingestion pipeline scripts across multi-disciplinary teams.

projects.title: E-Commerce Mesh Platform
projects.description: A high-performance, distributed e-commerce architecture designed to eliminate transactional checkout latency during peak traffic spikes. Built by decoupling legacy checkout micro-states and shifting execution pipelines to edge-cached gateway workers, the platform leverages a custom global WebSocket mesh for real-time data sync. This engineering overhaul compressed global analytical payloads by 42% and introduced strict multi-region fault isolation, ensuring sub-second execution latencies and zero-downtime reliability under heavy concurrent loads.

thumbnailImage.alt: E-Commerce Mesh Platform Project Capture Preview
